hypothesis_text: Using a robust floor-plan preprocessing pipeline (noise removal + wall detection confidence thresholds) will reduce distance-estimation errors on low-quality floor plans.
failure_mode: If distance estimation error remains high on low-quality plans, the hypothesis is false.
evidence_text: A key challenge in this domain is the Seating Allocation (SA) problem, which involves assigning teams to office space on a floor plan.

critic.suggestion: Feasibility: start with 3–5 toy floor plans; compare PRM/RRT distance error vs Euclidean/Manhattan as a proxy.

hypothesis_text: Adding a preference-aware term (e.g., contiguity/adjacency penalties) to the objective will improve human-rated seating quality without large optimization cost.
failure_mode: If human ratings do not improve or optimization cost increases too much, the hypothesis is false.
evidence_text: The usefulness between HSA and SA is significant to large organizations as the humanevaluated solution quality to SA would be low due to failure to accommodate linked departments.

critic.suggestion: Make it more testable: specify metric(s), baseline(s), and what counts as improvement.

hypothesis_text: Replacing the quadratic-variable formulation with a decomposition + heuristic warm-start will reduce runtime while preserving allocation quality on large instances.
failure_mode: If runtime does not decrease on large instances or allocation quality drops significantly versus baseline, the hypothesis is false.
evidence_text: A direct optimization approach could be employed to minimize the total seat distance between each seat, but this results in a mixed-integer quadratic problem (MIQP), which is not scalable for large instances due to the number of decision variables in the objective.

critic.suggestion: Feasibility: test on synthetic instances varying |S| and |T|; report runtime + quality vs IPSA baseline under a fixed time budget.
